title: Nharqis'I
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Nharqis'l system}} {{Nebula |image=[[File:NharqisI-Journal11.jpg]] |name=''Nharqis'I''
{{C|Depicted as seen through the ''Interrogator''{{'s}} viewport}} |region=[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} |sector=[[Veragi sector/Legends|Veragi sector]] |coordinates=K-2''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |xyz= |distance= |size= |formed= |system= |suns=None{{JournalCite|11|The Longest Fall}} |planets= |stations= |asteroids= |comets= |other= |routes= |distinctions=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |species= |otherspecies= |language= |government= |population= |interest= |imports= |exports= |affiliation= |battles1= |b1= |battles2= |b2= |battles3= |b3= |battles4= |b4= |battles5= |b5= |battles6= |b6= }} The '''''Nharqis'I''''' was a [[Color/Legends|black]]-colored [[Star/Legends|starless]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Veragi sector/Legends|Veragi sector]]. It was associated by many beings with [[Death/Legends|death]] and was said to be home to the mythical leviathan known as the ''[[Nharqis'Al]]''. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Interrogator/Legends|''Interrogator'']] was entering the nebula in order to investigate it. At that [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Jovan Vharing]], observed the ''Nharqis'I'' while waiting to meet Imperial [[High Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]] in the latter's waiting room. The nervous Vharing, expecting to be executed for the failure of his subordinate [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]], contemplated the nebula until the arrival of Tremayne, who then indeed promptly killed the captain. ==Description== The ''Nharqis'I'' was a little-known [[Star/Legends|starless]] and featureless [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] located in the [[Veragi sector/Legends|Veragi sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Its name, which shared similarities with the [[Olys Corellisi/Legends|Old Corellian]] phrases [[Nhar'qual Rift|''Nhar'qual'']]''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'' and "''Min min vil ut valle Nharqis''"{{JournalCite|7|Old Corellian: A Guide for the Curious Scholar}}—meaning "dark death" and "I will eat your ashes," respectively—was, despite its romantic appeal, a crude modification of a term in a lingering [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers']] dialect that referred to a [[Death/Legends|killing]] field{{CSWECite|II|377|Nharqis'I}} and was interpreted by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jovan Vharing]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to mean "the death place." Mythological beliefs associated with the ''Nharqis'I'' claimed the existence of the leviathan known as the ''[[Nharqis'Al]]'', who was said to lurk inside the nebula, and many beings associated the forbidding region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] with death, with it potentially reminding one of apparently endless continuity. ==History== [[File:JovanVharing.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Captain Jovan Vharing contemplated the ''Nharqis'I'' shortly before his death aboard the ''Interrogator'' while it was entering the region.]] In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of {{JournalCite|11|The Longest Fall}} to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. the Empire's [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Interrogator/Legends|''Interrogator'']] under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of Vharing moved in to investigate the ''Nharqis'I''. While the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] was maneuvering into the mouth of the nebula, the captain was observing the movement of the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|vessel]] through a [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] in the waiting room of Imperial [[High Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]], who had summoned Vharing after the latter's [[Senior Lieutenant/Legends|Senior Lieutenant]] [[Leeds]] had ordered a disastrous [[Attack on Qlothos|attack]] on the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Qlothos system/Legends|Qlothos system]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Qlothos]]. The nervous captain, who mused that he would be executed by Tremayne if the [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor's]] actions were as predictable as the dark void of the ''Nharqis'I'', wished that he could lose himself inside the nebula since he considered the waiting room even colder and more forbidding in comparison. When Tremayne finally entered the room, he did indeed promptly kill Vharing via the use of [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]]. While observing the black [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] of the Inquisitor, the dying captain imagined himself being drawn into the fabric and into an alternate world as dark and starless as the ''Nharqis'I''. Vharing subsequently had an after-death experience in which he, mistakenly believing himself to have survived the meeting with Tremayne, eventually found himself on the ''Interrogator''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. He observed the [[Starship/Legends|starship's]] crew preparing to leave the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], with areas of stars becoming visible through the portions of the receding nebula, and briefly wondered when the order to depart had been given before shrugging the thought away. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Nharqis'I'' first appeared in the short story "[[The Longest Fall]]," which was written by [[Patricia A. Jackson]], illustrated by [[Chris Trevas]], and published in the [[November]] [[1996]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|eleventh issue]] of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square K-2, although both the book and its [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] misspelled the nebula's name as the ''Nharqis'l'', with a lower-case "L." ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|11|The Longest Fall|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{ID|''Nharqis'l''}} *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} {{ID|''Nharqis'l''}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' {{ID|Nharqis'i Nebula}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Nharqis'I }} [[Category:Nebulae in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Veragi sector locations]]
